response.justification: Wei Chen is a capable full-stack and systems engineer with broad experience across TypeScript, Node.js, DLang, and web platforms, but is critically misaligned with this role's core requirements. Professional C++ experience amounts to approximately six months at National Instruments in 2014–2015, far short of the mandatory 5–10 years. There is no evidence of Windows-platform development, no SQL Server or Oracle exposure, and no fixed income, derivatives, or capital markets domain knowledge. The RIDE Capital stint involved fintech tooling in TypeScript/GraphQL, not capital markets systems. None of the mandatory criteria are met at the required depth.
strengths: Demonstrated software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) that would transfer well to a disciplined financial-systems environment
Experience with low-latency, high-throughput systems at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic provides relevant performance engineering mindset
Strong communication skills evidenced by public talks, workshops, and open-source project leadership
concerns: C++ experience is limited to roughly six months (National Instruments, 2014–2015) and a bachelor-thesis project — nowhere near the mandatory 5–10 years
No Windows-platform development history; work history is predominantly Linux and cloud-based environments
Zero fixed income, derivatives, investment banking, or capital markets domain exposure across 15+ years of career history

key_factors.factor: No capital markets / fixed income domain knowledge
